---
title: "UI_R027: Column/Field Display Name Format"
subtitle: "Standard format for displaying column names and field labels with abbreviations in zh_TW UI"
chapter: "CH04"
category: "rule"
number: "UI_R027"
date-created: "2026-03-10"
date-modified: "2026-03-10"
author: "Claude"
type: "rule"
law: "UI Components Law"
article-number:
  number: 27
  title: "Column/Field Display Name Format"
related_to:
  - "UI_R025: Taiwan Traditional Chinese Terminology"
  - "UI_R022: UI Text Translation Pattern"
  - "DEV_R052: English Keys in Business Logic"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article 27: Column/Field Display Name Format {#overview}

**UI Components Law Article 27 (Column/Field Display Name Format)**

This rule defines the standard display format for column names, field labels, KPI titles, and any data labels shown to users in the zh_TW locale. It resolves ambiguity when items have both a Chinese full name and an English abbreviation or code.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Rule
When a data item has a recognized English abbreviation or code, the display format is:

**`中文全稱（英文縮寫）`**

Chinese full name first, abbreviation in parentheses. This applies uniformly to all abbreviations including metric codes (CLV, RFM), status codes (E0, S1), and classification codes.
:::

# Section 1: Display Format Standard {#display-format}

## Subsection 1: Items with Abbreviations {#with-abbreviations}

**Format: `中文全稱（英文縮寫）`**

The Chinese full name comes first (because zh_TW is the primary UI language), followed by the English abbreviation in full-width parentheses `（）`.

**Examples:**

| English Key | zh_TW Display | Category |
|-------------|---------------|----------|
| CLV | 顧客終生價值（CLV） | Metric |
| RFM | 顧客價值分數（RFM） | Metric |
| NES | 新舊客狀態（NES） | Metric |
| CAI | 客戶活躍指數（CAI） | Metric |
| PCV | 預測客戶價值（PCV） | Metric |
| CRI | 客戶風險指數（CRI） | Metric |
| IPT | 平均購買間隔（IPT） | Metric |
| P(alive) | 存活機率（P(alive)） | Metric |
| E0 | 主力客（E0） | NES Status |
| S1 | 瞌睡客（S1） | NES Status |
| S2 | 半睡客（S2） | NES Status |
| S3 | 沈睡客（S3） | NES Status |
| N | 新客（N） | NES Status |
| RSV | RSV 分類（RSV） | Classification |
| MoM | 月增率（MoM） | Growth Rate |
| YoY | 年增率（YoY） | Growth Rate |

## Subsection 2: Items without Standard Abbreviations {#without-abbreviations}

**Format: Pure Chinese**

Items that do not have a recognized English abbreviation display in pure Chinese without parenthetical additions.

**Examples:**

| English Key | zh_TW Display |
|-------------|---------------|
| Average Churn Probability | 平均流失機率 |
| Total Revenue | 總營收 |
| Order Count | 訂單數 |
| Customer Count | 顧客數 |
| Average Order Value | 平均訂單金額 |

## Subsection 3: ID-Type Fields {#id-fields}

**Exception: ID-type fields may remain in English or use abbreviated format.**

| English Key | zh_TW Display | Reason |
|-------------|---------------|--------|
| Customer ID | 顧客 ID | ID is universally understood |
| Order ID | 訂單 ID | ID is universally understood |

# Section 2: Implementation {#implementation}

## Subsection 1: Translation Dictionary {#dictionary}

The `中文全稱（英文縮寫）` format is stored in `ui_terminology.csv`, not constructed at runtime.

**CSV entries:**

```csv
en_us,zh_tw
CLV,顧客終生價值（CLV）
RFM,顧客價值分數（RFM）
NES Status - E0,主力客（E0）
NES Status - S1,瞌睡客（S1）
```

## Subsection 2: Code Pattern {#code-pattern}

**Business logic remains in English (per DEV_R052). The format is applied only via `translate()` at the UI layer.**

```r
# Business logic: English keys (unchanged)
df$nes_status <- "E0"  # English code in data

# UI display: translate() returns formatted string
# translate("NES Status - E0") → "主力客（E0）"
tags$span(translate(paste0("NES Status - ", df$nes_status)))

# Table column names
colnames_display <- sapply(names(show_df), translate)
# translate("CLV") → "顧客終生價值（CLV）"
```

## Subsection 3: Why Chinese First {#rationale}

1. **Primary language**: The UI is in zh_TW; Chinese should lead
2. **Readability**: Users read the Chinese meaning first, then see the abbreviation for reference
3. **Consistency**: One uniform format eliminates ambiguity about display order
4. **Abbreviation aids comprehension**: Codes like E0/S1/S2/S3 convey ordering (0→3 = severity), and codes like CLV/RFM are widely used in marketing — omitting them loses information

# Section 3: Scope {#scope}

## Subsection 1: Where This Rule Applies {#applies-to}

- DT::datatable column names (via `sapply(names(df), translate)`)
- KPI card titles
- Chart axis labels and legends
- Sidebar filter labels
- Any user-visible field name or metric label

## Subsection 2: Where This Rule Does NOT Apply {#does-not-apply}

- Internal variable names (always English, per DEV_R052)
- Log messages and debug output
- API responses and CSV exports (always English)
- Code comments

# Section 4: Violations and Compliance {#violations}

**Violations:**

```r
# VIOLATION: Abbreviation only, no Chinese
colnames(show_df) <- c("CLV", "RFM", "NES")

# VIOLATION: Chinese only, missing abbreviation for items that have one
# translate("CLV") → "顧客終生價值"  (missing CLV in parens)

# VIOLATION: English first
# translate("CLV") → "CLV 顧客終生價值"  (wrong order)

# VIOLATION: NES status without code
# translate("NES Status - E0") → "主力客"  (missing E0 code)
```

**Compliant:**

```r
# COMPLIANT: Chinese first, abbreviation in parentheses
# translate("CLV") → "顧客終生價值（CLV）"
# translate("NES Status - E0") → "主力客（E0）"

# COMPLIANT: No abbreviation for items without standard codes
# translate("Total Revenue") → "總營收"
```

# Section 5: Principle Relationships {#relationships}

This rule:

1. **Extends UI_R025 (Taiwan Traditional Chinese Terminology)** — defines the display format for translated terms
2. **Works with UI_R022 (UI Text Translation Pattern)** — the format is stored in the translation dictionary
3. **Complements DEV_R052 (English Keys in Business Logic)** — business logic stays English; this rule governs the display layer only
4. **Resolves Issue #288** — clarifies the ambiguity about whether abbreviations like CLV should appear in English

---

*Based on: UI Components Law Article 27*
*Origin: Customer suggestion document 技詮儀表板問題_20260308.docx, Issue #288*
